title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple questions Dr. Kennedy regarding a young man who previously dated Helen. Dr. Kennedy dismisses the idea of a romantic connection, stating the man was merely an employee at the Fein and Wachtel Law Firm who left the firm after an indiscretion involving the revelation of his employer Mr. Walter Fein's affairs.

Elsewhere, Lily Gowan discusses a newspaper advertisement for a maid position at Dulcote St. Catherine's Cottage with her husband, Jim Gibb. Lily suspects the advertisement might be linked to a potential inheritance from Mrs. Harley or a reward for information regarding a murderer, recalling details involving a woman named Lorraine and a man's car. Jim, growing angry and bored, warns Lily not to meddle in such matters and leaves. Driven by greed and a desire for luxury, Lily decides to write to Dr. Harry, the brother of Lady Hildy, to seek advice, though she hesitates to mention details regarding Leonnie or a car.

The following morning, Miss Marple visits Jiles and Gwendolyn. She explains that she has hired an additional gardener, an old man named Manning, to assist Mr. Foster. Miss Marple reveals that Manning previously worked for Dr. Kennedy and identifies the young man who dated Helen as Avery. Jiles notes that he has obtained a handwriting sample of Helen's from Dr. Kennedy to send to an expert. The group meets Manning, a shrewd and grumpy hunchback, in the garden. As Manning discusses the history of local gardens and former residents like Mrs. Findlay, Jiles asks him if he indeed used to work for Dr. Kennedy.
